subject: ENG
course_description: Special topics in the study of Shakespeare. May include historical approaches, thematic emphases, performance studies, etc.
course_notes: (Same as THEA 317RW 1) (This course will look at a range of Shakespearean texts and productions, ranging from fairly traditional renditions to those that take the plays into unusual directions. We will be reading and discussing the plays, then delving into the wide range of ways that they can be presented to audiences. We will investigate drama presented by major companies, such as the Royal Shakespeare Company, Shakespeare's Globe and the National Theatre, but we will also investigate international and modernized productions and delve into the worlds of Shakespeare from perspectives of movement, music, and sound design. The course is intended for students who are new to Shakespeare, as well as for those with previous experience. All are welcome. Texts will be determined later, but a wide range of Shakespearean creations will be explored.)
